Wayne Rooney admits he would like to stay at Manchester United next season.
Birmingham goalkeeper Tomasz Kuszczak has signed a new two-year contract with the club.

Rafa Benitez has committed his future to Newcastle United manager following talks with owner Mike Ashley yesterday. The Spaniard was flown by helicopter to the Midlands' HQ of Sports Direct, where he met Ashley face-to-face.

Benitez signed a three-year deal last summer, but was unhappy with Newcastle's failure to sign Andros Townsend in the January transfer window.

Positive talks took place yesterday and Benitez has decided to continue as manager after guiding Newcastle to the Premier League at the first time of asking.

Benitez said: “I’m pleased with how the meeting went and the positive approach we are all taking together to build on what we have started this season.

“There will be challenges ahead of course, the summer will not be easy, but the hard work has been going on for some time and we can now continue positively with the development of the squad ahead of the start of the new season. I wish fans an enjoyable summer!”
Galatasaray want to sign out-of-favour Real Madrid defender Fabio Coentrao, according to sources in Portugal.

Liverpool midfielder Kevin Stewart is set to leave the club in the summer, Sky Sports News HQ understands.

The 23-year-old, who moved to Anfield in 2014 from Tottenham, has appeared nine times for the Merseyside club this season.

Sky sources claim a number of Premier League clubs are interested in taking him on loan or on a permanent deal, including Stoke, Burnley and newly promoted Brighton.

Derby, Norwich and Aston Villa are also thought to be interested in bringing Stewart to the Sky Bet Championship.
Everton captain Phil Jagielka admits he doesn't know if he'll be at Goodison Park next season.

The 18-year-old Norway international joined Heerenveen on an 18-month loan deal during the January transfer window.

“Madrid have the option to bring me back in June, but it seems like that is not going to happen,” he told Omrop Friesland. “The plan is for me to continue here next year, at Heerenveen.”

Lyon are set to spark a scramble for the signature of Alexandre Lacazette after admitting the player could be sold this summer.

Lacazette has been in sparkling form this season, scoring 26 goals in 29 Ligue 1 games and notching six Europa League goal in Lyon’s run to the semi-finals.

The 25-year-old France international striker has been a long-time rumoured target of Liverpool, with Arsenal, Barcelona, Borussia Dortmund and Atletico Madrid among other potential suitors.

And Lyon chairman Jean-Michel Aulas has admitted for the first time that Lacazette could move on during the forthcoming transfer window.

“If the club of Alex’s heart is capable of matching our demands, like Barcelona with Samuel Umtiti last year, then things will go well,” said Aulas.

“Otherwise, it also depends on any other outgoing transfers.

“If other players feel Lyon are not big enough for them, we will look for players who are equally strong and have the right mindset.

Alexandre Lacazette announces his departure from Lyon
Speaking in an exclusive interview with L’Équipe published in full tomorrow, Lyon striker Alexandre Lacazette announces his departure from the club, having been there since 2003.

You have decided to quit Lyon, in agreement with the club. Why are you leaving?

Because I think that now is the right moment. I want to discover something else, to put myself in danger and to get to the next level. People say in passing that I score goals, but I know very well that on the European level I am not very well known. I want to see if I can answer the questions that people are asking, to continue to progress.

We are hearing a lot about Atletico…

Atletico, they are good… This a club that is constantly in the final stage of the Champions League over the last few years. It is a great team, in a new stadium, and it is also a club where players progress. And there is Antoine Griezmann.

Who will decide your future destination?

Me. Entirely.

Even if a club offers Lyon more than the one that you want to go to?

The President told me what he told you in the press conference. I am the one who will choose my future destination. It is a personal choice that has been carefully considered for a long time.

Atletico Madrid make fresh check on Diego Costa and ask to be told of any China developments

Diego Costa - Diego Costa's future remains uncertain as Atletico Madrid want Chelsea striker to reject China and return to Spain
Diego Costa waves to supporters at Stamford Bridge on Sunday, but has he played his final league game for Chelsea? Credit: Rex Features
Atlético Madrid have expressed a desire to be kept informed of Chelsea striker Diego Costa’s intentions this summer, despite Tianjin Quanjian’s confidence of taking him to China.

Costa’s future at Chelsea remains uncertain with the 28-year-old possibly providing a hint he will leave by waving to each corner of Stamford Bridge when he was substituted in the final Premier League game of the season against Sunderland on Sunday.

Chinese Super League club Tianjin Quanjian are willing to pay £76 million to sign Costa and hand the Spain international a deal worth over £25m-a-year – figures Atlético or any other European club would not be able to or willing to match.

But Telegraph Sport understands Atlético, who tried to re-sign him last year, last week contacted the representatives of Costa to ask to be kept informed about what he wants to do this summer.

Diego Simeone has confirmed he will stay at Atlético and the Argentine manager is personally pushing the interest in Costa as the pair remain close. They were pictured together as recently as last November.

Atlético believe Costa would favour a return to Spain over a move to China, but it remains to be seen whether the player, his advisors or particularly Chelsea would accept a less lucrative deal for him to stay in Europe.

Tianjin Quanjian think they have a verbal agreement in place for Costa, but the player himself has suggested that he has not yet committed himself to moving to China and has told friends that he would be interested in a return to Spain.

Any Atlético bid for Costa will depend on whether or not the Spaniards sell Manchester United target Antoine Griezmann and would be subject to their transfer embargo being lifted.

Atlético already have a verbal deal in place to sign Lyon striker Alexandre Lacazette, but, with the contract of Fernando Torres up for renewal, the on-going interest in Costa is not dependent on whether or not Lacazette moves to Spain.

Representatives of Chelsea will this week meet with Monaco to try to negotiate a deal for midfielder Tiemoue Bakayoko, with the Ligue 1 champions interested in striker Michy Batshuayi.

Monaco want £50m for Bakayoko, while Chelsea are still undecided whether or not to let Batshuayi, who has scored in each of his last three appearances, leave – either on loan or permanently. Chelsea’s hopes of landing Bakayoko appear to have received a boost with the 22-year-old ruling out a move to Paris Saint-Germain.

“I can’t leave Monaco to go to PSG,” said Bakayoko. “It’s impossible, because I’ve just finished champion ahead of Paris. I can’t leave for a club that has finished behind me. “PSG is a club I carry in my heart because I’m a Parisian and I was a supporter of PSG, like my family. They initiated me.”

After playing two seasons at Juventus, Alvaro Morata is considering returning to Serie A. While his priority was to go back to the bianconeri, Mandzukic's recent extension has closed that door- but there's the possibility of joining Milan, a club looking to revamp their front line. Morata has a personal reason to return to Serie A, his future wife is Italian and he's now gathering information on the rossoneri's new project. Milan is becoming more optimistic they can complete the deal despite competition from Manchester United, Mourinho called Morata as he's interested in bringing him to Old Trafford but Morata's preference is to return to Italy with Milan giving him the chance to do so. The rossoneri's strategy is to first reach an agreement with Morata before negotiating with Real Madrid, after the Champions League final in Cardiff, directors Mirabelli and Fassone will travel to Spain to try to complete the deal for a key player for their attack.
